anzsic_codes.name: Automotive Body, Paint and Interior Repair

merger_description:
GIP V Snow Bidco Limited (the **Acquirer**) proposes to acquire 100% of the issued share capital of Envol Holdings Limited and its subsidiaries including TCR Group (the **Target**) (the **Acquisition**).

The Acquirer is a special purpose vehicle controlled by funds managed and/or advised by Global Infrastructure Management, LLC (together with is affiliates, **Global Infrastructure Management**).

Global Infrastructure Management is the global independent infrastructure fund manager of Global Infrastructure Partners (**GIP**), headquartered in New York, United States (**US**), and primarily focused on investing in transportation, energy, waste, water, and digital infrastructure sectors. Global Infrastructure Management also holds a 37% interest in Sydney Aviation Alliance Holdings Pty Ltd, which fully owns Sydney Airport Corporation Ltd. Global Infrastructure Management is ultimately controlled by BlackRock, Inc. (**BlackRock**).

BlackRock is a US company listed on the New York Stock Exchange. BlackRock is active in the provision of global investment management, risk management and advisory services to institutional and retail clients around the world.

The Target is the holding entity of TCR Group (**TCR**), which is primarily active in full-service leasing of ground support equipment (**GSE**) to independent ground handlers, airlines, and airports globally. TCR also provides repair and maintenance services for GSE (on both leased and third-party fleet) and fleet management services. TCR is headquartered in Steenokkerzeel, Belgium.

In Australia, TCR is involved in the leasing out and maintenance of GSE at airports around Australia through various subsidiaries.

TCR is currently indirectly solely controlled by 3i Group plc. 3i Group plc is an international investment company listed on the London Stock Exchange, with offices in Europe and North America.

events.title: Global Infrastructure Management - TCR – Phase 1 determination

determination_table_content.details:
GIP V Snow Bidco Limited proposes to acquire 100% of the issued
share capital of Envol Holdings Limited and its subsidiaries including
TCR Group.

The Australian Competition and Consumer Commission has
determined under section 51ABZE(1) of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) that the Acquisition may be put into effect.

The acquirer, GIP V Snow Bidco Limited (the Acquirer) proposes to
acquire 100% of the issued share capital of Envol Holdings Limited
and its subsidiaries including TCR Group (the Target)
(the Acquisition).
The Acquirer is a special purpose vehicle controlled by funds
managed and/or advised by Global Infrastructure Management, LLC
(together with is affiliates, Global Infrastructure Management).
Global Infrastructure Management is the global independent
infrastructure fund manager of Global Infrastructure Partners (GIP),
headquartered in New York, United States (US), and primarily
focused on investing in transportation, energy, waste, water, and
digital infrastructure sectors. Relevantly to the Acquisition, Global
Infrastructure Management also holds a 37% interest in Sydney
Aviation Alliance Holdings Pty Ltd, which fully owns Sydney Airport
Corporation Ltd. (Sydney Airport). Sydney Airport owns small
amounts of GSE with which it provides services to airlines. Global
Infrastructure Management is ultimately controlled by BlackRock,
Inc. (BlackRock).
BlackRock is a US company listed on the New York Stock Exchange.
BlackRock is active in the provision of global investment
management, risk management and advisory services to
institutional and retail clients around the world.
The target is the holding entity of TCR Group (TCR), which is
primarily active in full-service leasing of ground support equipment
(GSE) to independent ground handlers, airlines, and airports globally.
TCR also provides repair and maintenance services for GSE (on both
leased and third-party fleet) and fleet management services. TCR is
headquartered in Steenokkerzeel, Belgium.
In Australia, TCR is involved in the leasing out and maintenance of
GSE at airports around Australia through various subsidiaries.
TCR is currently indirectly solely controlled by 3i Group plc. 3i Group
plc is an international investment company listed on the London
Stock Exchange, with offices in Europe and North America.

determination_table_content.item:
Overlap and relationship
between the parties
determination_table_content.details:
There are vertical relationships between Global Infrastructure
Management and TCR relating to services at Sydney Airport. The
parties also overlap in the supply of GSE services to airlines at
Sydney Airport.

When making a determination in Phase 1, the Australian Competition
and Consumer Commission (ACCC) undertakes a competition
assessment and considers whether it is appropriate for an
acquisition to be approved or subject to further assessment in
Phase 2 in accordance with section 51ABZJ of the Competition and
Consumer Act 2010 (Cth) (the Act). In doing so, the ACCC must have
regard to the object of the Act and all relevant matters, including the
interests of consumers.
For more information about the ACCC’s approach to considering
notified acquisitions, see the ACCC’s merger assessment guidelines
and interim merger process guidelines.
In conducting its competition assessment, the ACCC has considered
the information and documents that were submitted with the
notification form, information submitted by third parties and publicly
available information.
The ACCC has determined that the Acquisition may be put into
effect as it considers that the Acquisition is unlikely to have the
effect of substantially lessening competition in any market. In
reaching its decision, and based on the material before it, the ACCC
makes the following findings.
• Noting a concern was raised by a third party that the Acquisition
may reduce competitors’ access to space at Sydney Airport, the
merged firm would be unlikely to have the ability to foreclose
rivals’ access to inputs at Sydney Airport as it does not have
sufficient control over the entities that ultimately control Sydney
Airport so as to foreclose rivals’ access and Sydney Airport
would not have the incentive to foreclose rivals’ access.
• The merged firm would likely continue to be constrained by
alternative suppliers of GSE services.

A notifying party, or other person who has been allowed to do so by
the Australian Competition Tribunal, may apply for review if they are
dissatisfied with the determination. Pursuant to section 100C of the
Act, applications for review of the determination are to be made to
the Australian Competition Tribunal before the end of 14 calendar
days after this statement of reasons was included on the ACCC’s
Acquisitions Register. To confirm whether there has been any
application for review, please contact the Australian Competition
Tribunal.
